issues.body: All three security-relevant changes are embedded workflow Python, but no test extracts and executes the changed self-claim, adopt, or live selected-model paths. The existing select-and-claim self-test extracts only the `Validate dry-run account against resolved policy` heredoc; it does not reach these branches. Consequently, restoring `if claimed_provider and ...` (and its harness/format equivalents), or removing either new missing-metadata loop, would not make a relevant assertion fail. For a trust-plane routing check, syntax validation alone is insufficient and leaves the fix unprotected against exactly the permissive regression it addresses.
issues.fix_hint: Add hermetic, non-vacuous tests against the real embedded workflow scripts. At minimum, run the live selected-model heredoc with valid protected routing and each of LIVE_PROVIDER, LIVE_HARNESS, and LIVE_CREDENTIAL_FORMAT empty in turn and assert a nonzero exit; include matching-value success and mismatched-value failure controls. Also exercise both claim-output heredocs with each routing metadata field missing/empty and assert rejection, plus a fully populated success fixture. Ensure extraction failure itself fails the test so the checks cannot silently skip when workflow text moves.
issues.title: Fail-closed routing changes have no regression test

verdict.summary: The new checks are directionally sound and fail closed: both claim paths reject absent metadata, and the final live-routing gate now requires exact equality. However, this trust-plane regression fix is not complete because the diff adds no non-vacuous test for any of those controls. Existing self-tests exercise the worker dry-run heredoc and unrelated workflow guards, not the self-claim/adopt parsing or the live selected-model branch changed here. A future reintroduction of the permissive truthiness guards could therefore pass the current suite.
